sub_mechanisms: M_1: each agent's message-type as an object in a category
M_2: braiding morphism between pairs of agent message-types
M_3: extract invariant subspace via center-of-category construction
M_4: agents exchange messages only through center-invariant projections
stripped_llm_application: Multi-agent communication where each agent's message type is treated as an object in a category and pairs of message types support a braiding morphism. The invariant subspace (the center-of-category construction) is extracted as the canonical 'common-ground' channel; agents exchange messages only via the projection onto this invariant subspace.
novelty_claim_stripped: LLM multi-agent communication uses pressure-fields (2601.08129), dynamic ad-hoc networking (2602.08009), or hierarchical coordination. None use categorical-center invariance to define the communication channel.
